title: (No AI) Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![Claude voice mode running on a phone.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Claude-Voice-2-3-up.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Until now, voice mode has only been available on Claude [Haiku](https://www.theverge.com/news/800007/anthropic-small-model-haiku-4-5), Anthropic's faster but less powerful model. Now the company is making its [Opus](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/913184/anthropic-claude-opus-4-7-cybersecurity) and [Sonnet](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/959686/anthropics-claude-sonnet-5-is-here) models available in [voice mode](https://www.theverge.com/news/649175/anthropic-claude-ai-voice-mode-report), and extending its reach into apps like Gmail, Slack, and Canva.

When Anthropic [launched voice mode](https://www.theverge.com/news/675330/anthropics-voice-mode-for-claude-launches-in-beta) last year, it was primarily focused on delivering answers to quick questions with minimal delay. But in a blog post, the company said people immediately started using voice mode for far more than casual queries. They were using it to work "through real business problems," which Haiku was not really designed for. That model "kept conversations quick, but not alwa …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/970065/anthropic-voice-mode-claude-opus-sonnet-haiku-ai)
contentSnippet:
Until now, voice mode has only been available on Claude Haiku, Anthropic's faster but less powerful model. Now the company is making its Opus and Sonnet models available in voice mode, and extending its reach into apps like Gmail, Slack, and Canva.
When Anthropic launched voice mode last year, it was primarily focused on delivering answers to quick questions with minimal delay. But in a blog post, the company said people immediately started using voice mode for far more than casual queries. They were using it to work "through real business problems," which Haiku was not really designed for. That model "kept conversations quick, but not alwa …
Read the full story at The Verge.

Now the company is making its Opus and Sonnet models available in voice mode, and extending its reach into apps like Gmail, Slack, and Canva. When Anthropic launched voice mode last year, it was primarily focused on delivering […]

title: (No AI) Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
author: Emma Roth
content:
![An illustration of the Patreon logo](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/STK326_PATREON_D.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Patreon is laying off 20 percent of its workers, or around 93 employees, as [reported earlier by _404 Media_](https://www.404media.co/patreon-lays-off-20-percent-of-its-workforce/). In a memo to employees, Patreon CEO Jack Conte [writes that the company](https://www.patreon.com/jackconte/posts/painful-update-164628951) isn't making these changes "because we believe AI replaces humans," but says AI has "fundamentally transformed the tech industry, including how we work, how we build products, how we communicate, and more."

That shift has changed "how we operate and organize," Conte writes.

During [an interview on _Decoder_, last month](https://www.theverge.com/podcast/952607/patreon-ceo-jack-conte), Conte laid out his stance on AI, saying that the company is "100 percent embracing" AI tools internally to continue serving creators:

> If Patreon doe …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/970211/patreon-layoffs-ai)

That shift has changed "how we operate and organize," Conte writes.
During an interview on Decoder, last month, Conte laid out his stance on AI, saying that the company is "100 percent embracing" AI tools internally to continue serving creators:

creator: Boone Ashworth
title: (No AI) Remember Jibo? Its Successor Is a Wearable That Turns Your Life Into AI Slop
dc:creator: Boone Ashworth
content: With “blessings” from the original Jibo founders, iKairos is a wearable or desk-mounted “AI journal” that turns your family moments into AI images and video.
contentSnippet: With “blessings” from the original Jibo founders, iKairos is a wearable or desk-mounted “AI journal” that turns your family moments into AI images and video.
